title: Get NLL(B) and NLL(S+B) from MultiDimFit

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I run
combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 --algo fixed --fixedPointPOIs mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --setParameters mean=27.4,width=2.6 --freezeParameters mean,width,r -v3
and I can access from output file
higgsCombineTest.MultiDimFit.mH120.root
deltaNLL  value.
Is there was that I can get separately NLL for B and NLL for S+B fit ?
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I run

combine -M MultiDimFit -d combined.txt --redefineSignalPOIs mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 --algo fixed --fixedPointPOIs mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --setParameters mean=27.4,width=2.6 --freezeParameters mean,width,r -v3

and I can access from output file

higgsCombineTest.MultiDimFit.mH120.root

deltaNLL value.

Is there was that I can get separately NLL for B and NLL for S+B fit ?

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi Sasha,
I believe you already asked this question here.
The information provided there is still true.
We may remove some of the offsets from combine, but making sure that all the offsets are removed will need a careful evaluation, and likely it is going to be useless as the statistical inference is usually based on likelihood ratios.
Do you have a specific use-case?
Cheers,
-Andrea
posts.cooked_html:

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Hi Andrea,
sometime we may have a local minimum in B or (and) S+B fits
and it might depend on the initial values of fitted parameters.
So having NLL separately one can recognize this situation and play around.
Cheers, Sasha.
